finding: Between Apr 4 and Apr 15, human-initiated sessions ran past 23:00 Stockholm time on 5 out of 10 active days (Apr 4, 7, 10, 11, 14). On Apr 7, work extended to 23:54; on Apr 14, active whisper-writer development continued until 00:29 on Apr 15. This is not a single late night but a recurring pattern of evening work bleeding past 23:00. The late-night-warn.py hook exists (triggers after 20:00 with 5+ hours active) but the pattern persists, suggesting the nudge either isn't surfacing or is being overridden by engagement momentum.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook exists in settings.json UserPromptSubmit — triggers after 20:00 with 5+ active hours. However, it has a 60-minute cooldown and doesn't escalate, so a single dismissal silences it for the rest of the night.

finding: Apr 11 stands out as a clear manic-mode day: 36 human sessions spanning 08:18 to 23:37 Stockholm time (15h19m), with only a ~3.5h afternoon break. The evening block alone ran 18:01-23:37 (5.5h continuous). Apr 12 followed with 34 human sessions spanning 08:38-19:34 (11h). Apr 14 added another 14h+ day (08:40 to 00:29 next morning). Three marathon days within a 4-day window matches the manic-mode signature: multiple sessions in rapid succession, scope expansion mid-day (NixOS migration, recursive self-improvement setup, whisper-writer fork all initiated on these days), and late-night continuation. The user's stated wellbeing goals explicitly include mindful breaks and not marathoning.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook addresses the tail end of marathon days but nothing flags the cumulative pattern across days or the total active hours within a single day before 20:00.

finding: On Apr 14, a new project (whisper-writer fork for streaming transcription) was initiated at 22:19 Stockholm time, then actively developed with 242 user messages until 00:14 — a 2-hour deep implementation session starting near midnight. On Apr 7, new hook development (run-it-yourself) was started at 23:10 and continued to 23:54. On Apr 9, a 226-message session started at 00:21. Starting new scope-expanding work in the late evening extends sessions well past natural stopping points because engagement momentum carries forward. This is distinct from wrapping up existing work — it's initiating fresh creative/implementation work when winding down would better serve wellbeing goals.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py fires after 20:00 but by 22:00+ the user is already deep in a new task. The hook doesn't distinguish between continuing existing work and starting new scope.

finding: On Apr 14 at 22:43 Stockholm time, the user asked 'Is Autodoro running? I think I've been here for a long while and I have not gotten any message.' This suggests the user's own break-reminder tool (Autodoro) was not functioning as expected during a marathon session. The user noticed the gap themselves, indicating awareness of the pattern but insufficient tooling support. Autodoro was also being configured to exclude whisper-writer from its detection, which may have reduced its effectiveness.
existing_mitigation: Autodoro exists as a break reminder but was apparently not running or not triggering during this extended session. The user was actively aware something was wrong.
